mechanism_narrative: Rising real activity in retail sales signals improving household demand and near-term earnings momentum, so higher 3-month growth in RSAFS predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the 3-month RSAFS growth signal has Rank IC below 0.03 in any 3-year walk-forward window or if top-quintile signal months do not outperform bottom-quintile months over 20 trading days.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate crossing above 1.0% from below marks a tightening transition that raises equity discount rates and reduces risk appetite, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses above 1.0% after being below 1.0% in the prior month
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if mean triggered returns are not below unconditional mean returns at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A fast increase in unemployment signals deteriorating labor income and recession risk, so a 3-month UNRATE rise greater than 0.3 percentage points predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered instances have nonnegative average 60-day forward returns or if downside hit rate is below 55% across two consecutive 3-year walk-forward windows.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation acceleration combined with rising policy rates creates a valuation headwind because expected tightening rises faster than nominal cash-flow support, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month percent change accelerates versus the prior 3 months and FEDFUNDS increases over the same 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if the joint acceleration signal does not underperform the unconditional 20-day return mean or if the interaction term has the wrong sign in walk-forward regressions for two consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production below its recent trend signals weakening output and operating leverage, so a downward cross of INDPRO below its 6-month moving average predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO crosses below its 6-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if downward-cross triggers have positive average 60-day returns or if a short INDPRO trend signal fails to beat a zero-signal benchmark in more than half of walk-forward folds.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in housing starts while retail sales remain firm indicates a leading cyclical slowdown hidden by coincident consumption, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 6-month percent change is below -10% while RSAFS 3-month percent change is positive
failure_condition: Reject if the housing-retail divergence trigger has higher 60-day returns than periods with both HOUST and RSAFS rising, or if downside hit rate is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll creation with unemployment below 5% signals resilient income growth and low recession risk, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days through improved risk appetite.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month net change exceeds 450 thousand and UNRATE is below 5.0%
failure_condition: Reject if triggered cases do not have a positive average 5-day return or if positive hit rate is below the unconditional 5-day hit rate in two consecutive walk-forward windows.

mechanism_narrative: Positive real GDP growth confirms broad demand expansion and corporate revenue support, so higher quarter-over-quarter growth in GDPC1 predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth crosses above 2.0%
failure_condition: Reject if GDPC1 growth-above-2% triggers fail to produce positive average 60-day returns or if their mean return is not higher than below-2% growth periods at p < 0.10.
